user_task: Standalone visual-document extraction (PDF / DOCX / PPTX) — text, tables, charts, infographics, pictures.
source: JTBD & user tasks tab, EXTRACT bucket; seed queries from 'NeMo Retriever — JTBD seed queries' tab, EXTRACT → standalone visual-document extraction (10-K PDF revenue lookup, PDF climate-risk page references, QBR pptx revenue/pipeline).

success_criteria.quality: operational pass — ingest one visual document (PDF/DOCX/PPTX) and get back a non-zero number of chunks; the visual pipeline (nemotron-page-elements-v3 -> nemotron-ocr-v2 -> nemotron-table-structure-v1) fires; tables surface as discrete content_type=table rows with row/col structure; picture/chart regions are detected and either captioned or stored as image content; NO silent text-only fallback when tables/charts are present. Binary pass/fail.
success_criteria.time: medium — <= 2 min per case (single small file ingest + one query).
success_criteria.trigger_rate: >= 95% — an 'extract / load this document and answer from it' prompt must fire the skill
success_criteria.subcommand_accuracy: >= 90% — must run `retriever ingest <file>` (format auto-detected; tables/charts on by default; --use-table-structure to materialize row/col table structure) then `retriever query` with --content-types text,table to confirm table rows; no --input-type flag (does not exist)

environment.format_autodetect: `retriever ingest <file>` auto-detects PDF/DOCX/PPTX from the extension. There is NO --input-type flag.

prereqs.libreoffice: .docx / .pptx are converted to PDF by libreoffice (host pkg: `sudo apt-get install -y libreoffice`) and then run through the SAME pdf extraction branch — `--dry-run` shows branch_summary `pdf:1` for a single .docx or .pptx, i.e. they route through the pdf family. Without libreoffice, DOCX/PPTX ingest fails.
prereqs.visual_extraction_endpoints: page-elements / OCR / table-structure run on hosted ai.api.nvidia.com by default (API key from NVIDIA_API_KEY) OR on a local GPU when configured. This EXTRACT suite is host-agnostic: the assertion is that the visual stack fired and produced table/picture rows, not WHERE it ran.

extraction_defaults_from_dry_run.note: Tables/charts/page-elements extraction is ON by default. use_table_structure is OFF by default; passing --use-table-structure flips it true and switches table_output_format to markdown, materializing nemotron-table-structure-v1 row/col output so table cells are queryable as discrete content_type=table rows.

environment.ingest_success_line: Ingested N file(s) -> M row(s) in LanceDB lancedb/<table>.
environment.query_hit_shape: JSON array; each hit has exactly {source, page_number, text}; page_number is a 1-indexed int.

cases.title: Baseline: extract a multi-page visual PDF -> non-zero chunks, answer from body text
cases.complexity_axis: Satisfies the EXTRACT operational-pass core (ingest 1 file -> non-zero chunks) in its simplest form: a single multi-page PDF with text, tables, charts and a picture. Rung 1 only asserts the file ingests and produces a non-zero row count and a grounded text answer — it does not yet drill into a specific table cell. Adds nothing over a prior rung (it is the floor).
cases.prompt: Extract data/multimodal_test.pdf and tell me what the document says it should contain by the end (how many tables, charts, and bullet points).
expected_commands: $RETRIEVER query "How many tables, charts, and bullet points should be extracted?" --top-k 5

expected_output.ingest_summary_line: Ingested 1 file(s) -> N row(s) in LanceDB lancedb/nemo-retriever. (branch_summary pdf:1, N >= 1)
expected_output.pipeline: visual pipeline fires: nemotron-page-elements-v3 -> nemotron-ocr-v2 over the 3 pages (extract_text/tables/charts/images all true by default)
expected_output.query_shape: JSON array of hits; each {source, page_number, text}; >= 1 hit citing multimodal_test.pdf
expected_output.answer: The conclusion (p3) states it should extract 2 tables, 2 charts, and 3 bullet points; cite multimodal_test.pdf p3

pass_when: `retriever ingest` returns a NON-ZERO row count (chunks produced) for multimodal_test.pdf
query returns >= 1 grounded hit citing multimodal_test.pdf
answer reflects '2 tables, 2 charts, 3 bullet points' (grounded in p3 conclusion)
no --input-type flag used (does not exist); format auto-detected as pdf

cases.title: PDF with a TABLE -> recover a specific cell as a content_type=table row
cases.complexity_axis: Satisfies the validation path's 'tables surface as discrete content_type=table rows with row/col structure' clause. Adds over rung 1: instead of body text, the answer must come from a TABLE cell, which forces --use-table-structure (nemotron-table-structure-v1) and a query filtered to --content-types text,table. New dimension: table-structure materialization + table-typed retrieval; catches a silent text-only fallback.
cases.prompt: From data/table_test.pdf, what value did James have in 2019? I need it pulled from the table, not guessed.

expected_output.ingest_summary_line: Ingested 1 file(s) -> N row(s) in LanceDB lancedb/nemo-retriever. (table rows present)
expected_output.pipeline: --use-table-structure -> use_table_structure true + table_output_format markdown; nemotron-table-structure-v1 fires after page-elements + OCR v2
expected_output.table_row_assertion: at least one returned hit is a table-derived row (content_type=table) carrying the cell; no silent text-only fallback

pass_when: ingest runs with --use-table-structure so table-structure (nemotron-table-structure-v1) materializes row/col cells
query uses --content-types text,table (valid value) and returns a table row
answer is exactly 978 citing table_test.pdf p1
fails if the table is flattened to plain text only (silent text fallback) and 978 cannot be recovered as a table cell

cases.title: DOCX (same content) -> format routing through libreoffice -> pdf branch
cases.complexity_axis: Satisfies the 'DOCX' modality of the EXTRACT task. Adds over rung 2 exactly one dimension: a non-PDF source format. The .docx is auto-detected and converted by libreoffice to PDF, then run through the SAME pdf extraction branch (dry-run shows branch_summary pdf:1). New dimension: format routing + the libreoffice host prereq; same multimodal content/ground truth as the PDF so the variable is purely the input format.
cases.prompt: I only have the Word version: extract data/multimodal_test.docx and tell me, from Table 1, what activity the Giraffe is doing and where.
expected_commands: $RETRIEVER query "What activity is the Giraffe doing and where (Table 1)?" --top-k 5 --content-types text,table

expected_output.ingest_summary_line: Ingested 1 file(s) -> N row(s) in LanceDB lancedb/nemo-retriever. (branch_summary pdf:1 — docx converted to pdf via libreoffice, then pdf extraction branch)
expected_output.routing: format auto-detected as docx; libreoffice converts .docx -> pdf; dry-run branch_summary is pdf:1 (routes through the pdf family)
expected_output.answer: Giraffe — Driving a car — At the beach; cite multimodal_test.docx (Table 1)

pass_when: ingest auto-detects .docx (no --input-type) and produces NON-ZERO rows
docx is routed through the pdf branch (libreoffice conversion); requires libreoffice host pkg
Table 1 cell recovered: Giraffe is 'Driving a car' 'At the beach' (content_type=table)
answer grounded in the docx, matching the multimodal PDF ground truth
cases.known_caveat: DOCX/PPTX extraction requires the libreoffice host package (`sudo apt-get install -y libreoffice`). Without it, the .docx -> pdf conversion step fails and ingest cannot produce chunks. This is the prereq the rung is built to exercise.

cases.title: PPTX slide deck -> table/chart from a slide (chart-region extraction)
cases.complexity_axis: Satisfies the 'PPTX' modality and the 'charts / infographics' clause. Adds over rung 3: a slide-deck source whose key figures live in a CHART (slide 3 chart, not body text) plus a text KPI slide. The chart region must be detected and its values surfaced (captioned or as chart/table content) so a chart-backed answer is possible. New dimension: chart-region extraction from a presentation; same libreoffice->pdf routing as docx.
cases.prompt: From data/aurora_qbr_q1.pptx, what's the FY27 Q1 revenue projection, and which region has the largest pipeline?
expected_commands: $RETRIEVER query "FY27 Q1 revenue projection and region with the largest pipeline" --top-k 5 --content-types text,table,chart

expected_output.ingest_summary_line: Ingested 1 file(s) -> N row(s) in LanceDB lancedb/nemo-retriever. (branch_summary pdf:1 — pptx converted to pdf via libreoffice)
expected_output.routing: format auto-detected as pptx; libreoffice converts .pptx -> pdf; charts on by default (extract_charts true)
expected_output.answer: FY27 Q1 revenue projection = $487M (+18% YoY) (slide 2 'Revenue Trajectory'); largest pipeline region = Americas at $1,200M / $1.2B (slide 3 'Pipeline by Region' chart: Americas 1200, EMEA 480, APAC 420)
expected_output.chart_assertion: slide-3 chart region detected; the Americas=1200 figure is recoverable (chart/table/caption content), not silently dropped

pass_when: ingest auto-detects .pptx (no --input-type), routes via libreoffice->pdf, NON-ZERO rows
text KPI recovered: FY27 Q1 revenue projection = $487M (slide 2)
chart-backed answer recovered: Americas has the largest pipeline (1200 vs EMEA 480, APAC 420; slide 3 chart)
chart region is detected/extracted (no silent text-only fallback that loses the chart figures)
cases.known_caveat: PPTX requires libreoffice (same as DOCX). Chart numeric values come from a chart object; surfacing them depends on chart extraction (extract_charts, on by default) and/or VLM captioning (--caption). If charts are dropped, the Americas=1200 figure is unrecoverable — which this rung is designed to catch.

cases.title: Acceptance: visual-rich PDF where text + table + chart + picture all extract, with page/bbox citations
cases.complexity_axis: Composed operational-pass / acceptance gate for the EXTRACT row. Adds over rung 4: a single visual-rich document where ALL four modalities (body text, a table cell, a chart, a picture region) must independently land and be provable via per-modality queries, each hit carrying source_id + page_number (and bbox_xyxy_norm where applicable). Asserts NO silent text-only fallback and that extraction-only was requested (no separate index/embed step is the user's ask — the ingest+query loop simply proves chunks of every modality exist). This is the gate the others build up to.
cases.prompt: Do a full visual extraction of data/multimodal_test.pdf and prove every modality landed: (1) the body text of the Introduction, (2) the Giraffe row of Table 1, (3) what Chart 1 is about, and (4) that the page with the 'high-quality picture of some shapes' was captured. Give me page references for each.
expected_commands: $RETRIEVER query "Giraffe activity and place in Table 1" --top-k 5 --content-types text,table
$RETRIEVER query "high-quality picture of some shapes" --top-k 5 --content-types text,image,infographic

expected_output.ingest_summary_line: Ingested 1 file(s) -> N row(s) in LanceDB lancedb/nemo-retriever. (N >= 1; rows of multiple content types)
expected_output.pipeline: full visual stack: nemotron-page-elements-v3 -> nemotron-ocr-v2 -> nemotron-table-structure-v1 (via --use-table-structure); charts + images + infographics on by default

answers.text: Introduction: placeholder document with headings/placeholder text used for testing; cite multimodal_test.pdf p1
answers.table: Giraffe -> Driving a car -> At the beach (Table 1); content_type=table; cite multimodal_test.pdf p1
answers.chart: Chart 1 shows some gadgets and their (fictitious) costs; cite multimodal_test.pdf p1
answers.picture: the picture of shapes is referenced on p2 ('Below, is a high-quality picture of some shapes'); a picture/image region is captured on that page

expected_output.citation_assertion: every returned hit carries non-null source (source_id) + page_number; table/chart/picture rows carry bbox_xyxy_norm where applicable

pass_when: single ingest of multimodal_test.pdf produces NON-ZERO rows spanning multiple content types (text + table + chart + image)
text modality: Introduction body text recovered (p1)
table modality: Giraffe row recovered as a content_type=table row (Driving a car / At the beach, p1)
chart modality: Chart 1 ('gadgets and costs') recoverable (chart/caption content, p1) — not silently dropped
picture modality: a picture/image region is captured (p2 'picture of some shapes')
every hit carries source + page_number; NO silent text-only fallback that loses table/chart/picture
